reasoning.typography: Generic sans-serif (likely system font) with no character — could be any VC site. The type system shows no distinctive personality or disciplined scale.
reasoning.hierarchy: Headline dominates and does visual work, but the carousel dot navigation and attribution compete. Single focus per screen mostly achieved through image size.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Generic VC language ('New defense for a new terrain: adapt, design, deploy') + logo + carousel = completely interchangeable. Anduril credit doesn't save it from stencil status.
reasoning.color: Restrained grayscale monochrome with small accent (flag logo uses red/blue). Disciplined and serious, avoids decorative excess.
reasoning.density: Large hero image with minimal text shows confidence, but it's the generic VC confidence — not earned through content structure. Uniform padding around elements.
reasoning.voice: The headline attempts gravitas but 'adapt, design, deploy' is consultant-speak. Structure appears to be standard carousel → portfolio grid. No evident IA reflecting actual story.

winning_patterns: Restrained monochromatic palette creates serious/institutional tone
Large hero image with minimal text shows confidence in presentation
Clean logo mark with geometric simplicity
anti_patterns: Carousel is the 2015 stencil — lazy information architecture
Generic headline could work for any defense/tech VC or consulting firm
Typography has zero personality — system font mediocrity
Portfolio company name as attribution doesn't create distinctiveness
No evidence of structural thinking beyond 'hero → carousel → presumably grid below'
